title: Held book drifts to ~47% crypto variance share intra-week (cap enforced only at rebalance)
source: Surfaced by the B0118 drift-aware validation (scripts/sweep_no_trade_band_driftaware.py): tracking the HELD book's stressed crypto variance share daily revealed it peaks at ~0.473 intra-week at band=0.00 (i.e. INDEPENDENT of B0118) — the constant-weight backtest hid it.
why: The crypto_var_cap (0.30) is imposed by compute_survival_target only at the WEEKLY rebalance (on the target). Between rebalances the held weights drift with price; a crypto rally pushes the held stressed crypto variance share to ~47% — well over the 30% cap — until the next weekly rebalance trims it. The fast risk_check() only does HARD kills (DD latch, daily-loss), never a soft cap-trim. So the deployed book runs materially more crypto-concentrated intra-week than the audited cap implies.
value_rationale: M: a real, previously-hidden gap between the audited (rebalance-time) risk profile and the realized (intra-week) one; matters for a preservation book in the weekend-crypto-gap regime risk_check was built for. Not H because maxDD held up in the drift-aware sim (the gap-aware kill still bounds the tail) and it is pre-existing, not a regression.
scope: Quantify how often / how far the held crypto var share exceeds 0.30 intra-week (drift-aware sim).
Option A: have the fast risk_check() trim crypto back to the cap intra-week (not only hard-kill).
Option B: rebalance more often when held crypto var share breaches (event-driven, like the B0118 held-cap bypass but on its own cadence).
Decide with the risk-officer whether ~47% intra-week is acceptable for the mandate or must be capped.
recommendation: Investigate with the risk-officer. The B0118 held-cap bypass already forces a rebalance at the next WEEKLY check when held breaches; B0119 is whether that weekly cadence is fast enough for crypto, or the fast path must enforce the cap intra-week.
out_of_scope: The no-trade band itself (B0118 — this drift exists at band=0); the hard kill-switches.

intra-week crypto var share quantification (drift-aware, deployed config)

history.reason: QUANTIFIED (scripts/preflight_intraweek_crypto_var.py, drift-aware, deployed band=0.02). Over the SOL-complete recent window (the most crypto-concentrated, conservative): held stressed crypto var share is CENTERED AT THE CAP (time-wtd mean 0.295, p50 0.293), with a MODEST right tail (p95 0.358 = +19%) and RARE peaks (max 0.473 = +58%). 35% of days > 0.30, 4% > 0.40. Breach episodes mean 3.5d, MAX 32d (a sustained crypto rally outpacing weekly trims). NOT 'materially over-cap most of the time' — centered on the cap. NOTABLE: the B0118 band (0.02) IMPROVES concentration vs baseline (35% vs 52% of days > 0.30) because the held-cap bypass actively trims crypto on breach. The kills bound the downside tail regardless (run_backtest crypto-era: 0 kills, 0 dd-stops, maxDD -5.12%). Earlier kill-confound hypothesis REFUTED (kill/no-kill runs identical -> kills never fired; the 478 'days' is share-COMPUTABILITY, not exposure — the book is invested all 1824 days). Open decision for the risk-officer: accept+document (mild, centered, kills bound tail, band improves it) vs tighten the rebalance crypto cap (cheap, shifts p95 toward 0.30) vs intra-week enforcement in risk_check (heavy; covers the weekend-crypto gap).
research(survival): B0119 quantify intra-week crypto var drift â€” centered at the cap

history.reason: RESOLVED via Option B (CEO 2026-05-31, risk-officer PROCEED_WITH_CAVEAT). Lowered the deployed crypto_var_cap 0.30 -> 0.25 (scripts/run_survival_live.py --crypto-var-cap default 0.25 -> SurvivalBookConfig.crypto_var_cap; dry-run confirms target crypto_var_share=0.25). VERIFIED re-centering (preflight at 0.25): held stressed crypto var share p95 0.358 -> 0.307 (onto the advertised ~0.30), max 0.473 -> 0.414, >0.40 days 4.0% -> 0.4% (10x). The cap is a REBALANCE-TIME TARGET, not an intra-week hard limit; documented in docs/estrategia-live-explicada.md (section 4). Risk-officer findings: NOT a ruin risk (the DD latch + gap-aware kill bound the downside tail INDEPENDENT of concentration — they fire on loss magnitude; run_backtest crypto-era 0 kills, maxDD -5.12%); the overshoot degraded expected diversification, not the worst-case floor. Option C (intra-week trim in risk_check) REJECTED — would add an order-PLACING path to the close-only fast kill tick. risk_check unchanged. Reversible via --crypto-var-cap 0.30. NOTE: B0112 bootstrap/stress validated at 0.30; 0.25 is the SAFE direction (less crypto variance), no re-validation required.
feat(survival): B0119 lower crypto var cap 0.30 -> 0.25 (re-center realized concentration)
